mappings.rationaleAI: Electrostatics (A), electric currents (B), magnetism (C), field theory (D), electromagnetic radiation theory (E), and relativistic electrodynamics (F).
Static electricity (A), direct current (B.1), conduction mechanisms (B.2), and alternating current (B.3).
General phenomena (C.1), magnetic effects of currents (C.2), charged particles in fields (C.3), electromagnetic induction (C.4), and magnetic materials (C.5).
Classical radiation theory including Maxwell's equations (E.1) and quantum theory including Compton and photoelectric effects (E.2).
Maxwell's equations for electromagnetic nature of light (E.1.b) and mathematical formulation of electromagnetic theory (E.3.a).
Faraday's law (B.3.a), laws of electromagnetic induction (C.4.a), and electrolysis (B.5.d).
Charged particles in electric fields (B.2.a), electron emission (B.2.d), and motion in magnetic and electric fields (C.3).
Particle nature of radiation (E.2.a) and wave-particle duality (E.2.b).
Quantum theory of radiation (E.2) and relation to quantum theory and relativity (E.2.d).
AC systems (B.3) and generation/transmission of AC power (B.3.k).
Batteries providing electromotive force (B.1.b).
Electromagnetic field force and energy (B.4.c) and magnetic effects of currents (C.2).
Semiconductor conduction (B.2.b) and AC circuit principles (B.3).
Nature of light (E.1.a) and optical effects (B.5.b).
Photoelectric effect (E.2.a) and relativistic electrodynamics (F).
Conduction in semiconductors (B.2.b).
Nuclear magnetic moments and magnetic resonance (C.3.d).
Conduction in gases involving ionization (B.2.b).
Conduction in vacuum via electron emission (B.2.b).

Thermoelectric effects (B.2.c).
